title: Dog Decreased Appetite and Lethargy: Triage Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: Is your dog tired and refusing food? Learn about the potential causes of lethargy and decreased appetite and when to contact your veterinarian.
content_html:
TL;DR: Lethargy and loss of appetite are signs of an underlying health issue that typically requires a veterinary examination within 24 hours to prevent dehydration and further complications.

What does it mean if my dog is lethargic and not eating?

When a dog shows both a decreased appetite and lethargy, it is a sign that their body is diverting energy to fight off an underlying health issue. These are known as non-specific symptoms, meaning they can be associated with a wide range of conditions, including minor stomach upsets, infections, dental pain, or more chronic internal issues.

Is my dog's lethargy and loss of appetite a veterinary emergency?

  • The urgency level for these symptoms is Medium; schedule a veterinary visit within 24 hours if your dog is still drinking water.
  • Seek immediate care if your dog experiences severe symptoms like collapse or respiratory distress.
  • Do not wait more than a day for an evaluation, as persistent refusal to eat can lead to weakness and dehydration, especially in smaller breeds or senior dogs.

How can sharing photos or videos help with my dog's triage?

  • Providing media to a triage professional significantly improves the accuracy of a remote assessment.
  • Share a video of your dog's breathing or movement to help determine the severity of their lethargy.
  • Send clear photos of your dog's gums (which should be pink) or any abnormal vomit or stool to provide clues about their circulation and digestive health.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

In dogs presenting with decreased appetite and lethargy, differential diagnoses should include hepatopathy and gastrointestinal neoplasia/malabsorption. Additional signs that may be present include diarrhea, weight loss, and vomiting. Clinicopathologic findings associated with hepatopathy may include modestly increased liver enzyme activity, hypoalbuminemia, hypocholesterolemia, and increased total bile acids in the absence of hyperbilirubinemia. Animals with malabsorption may present with dehydration, anemia, ascites, or edema in cases of hypoproteinemia; thickened bowel loops or enlarged mesenteric lymph nodes may be palpable. Electrolyte and acid-base disturbances, such as hypochloremia, hypokalemia, and metabolic alkalosis or acidosis, may also be present, especially if vomiting is ongoing.

Chapter: Gastroenterology, Hepatology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 465)

faqs.question: How long can a dog go without eating?
faqs.answer: While some healthy dogs can go 24 hours without a meal, a dog that is also lethargic should be evaluated by a veterinarian as soon as possible to prevent complications.

faqs.question: Should I try to tempt my dog with human food?
faqs.answer: You can try offering a small amount of plain, boiled chicken or white rice, but avoid fatty or seasoned foods which can worsen the condition.

faqs.question: What are the signs that this has become an emergency?
faqs.answer: Seek immediate emergency care if your dog has pale gums, is unable to stand, is panting excessively while at rest, or is vomiting uncontrollably.
